2005-03-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Ready To Vote On Constitution
2005-03-01.abstract: French Parliament clears way for referendum on European Union's new consitution; Pres Jacques Chirac will meet with leaders to determine date for vote; eight other countries will vote on constitution, while remaining nations will ratify document by parliamentary vote (S)

2005-03-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Hidden Daughter Speaks Out
2005-03-01.abstract: Mazarine Pingeot, illegitimate daughter of former French President Francois Mitterrand who was hidden from public until 1994, publishes book Bouche Cousue, Sealed Lips; Mitterrand died in 1996; photo (S)

2005-03-01.title: Syria's President Tells Magazine That Troops Will Leave Lebanon
With New Chief, Uruguay Veers Left, in a Latin Pattern
2005-03-01.abstract: Uruguay will join other Latin American nations in new leftist consensus as it prepares to inaugurate Tabare Vazquez as president; three-quarters of nations are governed by left-leaning leaders, all of whom have emerged in past six years and are redefining what left means; tide is not so much doctrinaire as pragmatic; leaders are different in style and substance, but united in conviction that free-market reforms of 1990's have failed and by renewed focus on egalitarianism and social welfare; attitude toward United States is one of polite distancing, as economic reforms being rejected are closely associated with Washington and financial institutions it backs; critics on left decry middle ground as sellout but others see it as maturing into mainstream; photos (M)

2005-03-01.title: Tsunami aid arriving with rare speed

2005-03-02.title: 2 Members of Hussein Tribunal Are Assassinated in Baghdad
2005-03-02.abstract: Iraqi judge and lawyer with special tribunal that will try Saddam Hussein and former members of his government are shot to death outside their home in Baghdad; Parwiz Muhammad Mahmoud al-Merani and son, who worked at tribunal, are killed day after first charges are announced; senior Iraqi official says Hussein's half brother was captured by Iraqi and allied forces, not by Syria; some 400 tribunal members have security guards and names are kept secret; seven Iraqi police officers are killed in four incidents, day after bombing in Hilla that killed at least 122 people and wounded 170 more; photo; Abu Musab al-Zarqawi terrorists claim responsibility; Muslim Scholars Assn, influential Sunni group, denounces Hilla attack; French reporter Florence Aubenas, kidnapped Jan 5, appeals for help on videotape (M)

2005-03-02.title: U.N. Conference and U.S. in Conflict Over Abortion Language
Bush Says World Demands That Syria Pull Out of Lebanon

2005-03-03.title: Nations offer support for a Palestinian state
Indonesian cleric's case puts more than accused on trial
News Analysis: India's budget aimed at both the elite and the rural majority
Beijing official visits Seoul to push talks with North

2005-03-04.title: Magnate arrested in Japan in fraud case
Radical Cleric in Indonesia Is Acquitted of Terrorism
2005-03-04.abstract: Indonesian court acquits radical Islamic cleric Abu Bakar Bashir of all terrorism charges in bombings in Marriott Hotel in Jakarta in Aug 2003, and of having directed bombings of nightclubs in Bali in Oct 2002; for more than two years, Bush administration has pressed Indonesian government to prosecute man they portray as Osama bin Laden's lieutenant in Southeast Asia; in what appears to be concession to American pressure, five judges convict Bashir of one of eight counts, criminal conspiracy in Bali bombings, saying his words may have encouraged perpetrators, whom he knew; prosecution's case was hampered because Bush adminstration refused to make available to Indonesians two importat al Qaeda members now in American custody, Riudan Isamuddin and Omar al-Faruq (M)

2005-03-04.title: Prime minister says he will resign in May
A Shaken France Watches as a Vast Pedophile Trial Begins
2005-03-04.abstract: Pedophile case involving 66 adults accused of abusing 45 children opens in Angers, France, setting off national debate about erosion of moral values and state's ability to protect underprivileged children; trial involves 39 men and 27 women charged with rape or selling their children for sex; nearly all come from French underclass; case came to light while police were monitoring convicted sex offender; lawyers say case is much stronger than sordid pedophile trial of 18 people in city of Outreau year ago; that case ended with suicide and 10 convictions after central defendant suddenly retracted her story implicating other defendants in abuse of at least 18 children, leaving many people uncertain of guilt of some of those convicted (M)

2005-03-05.title: From North Korea, a new puzzle
Sinn Fein suspends 7 over Belfast stabbing
For many women, a lost decade
Syria May Announce Partial Pullout of Troops
2005-03-05.abstract: Syria says that Pres Bashar al-Assad will convene session of Parliament to deliver speech, raising expectations that he will announce partial pullout of troops from Lebanon in effort to ease growing international pressure on his government (M)

2005-03-05.title: Italian is freed in Iraq and is hit by U.S. fire

2005-03-06.title: Hezbollah Declares Full Support for Syria
U.N. Official Says Sudan Is Not Getting the Aid It Was Promised
Unexpected Whiff of FreedomProves Bracing for the Mideast
2005-03-06.abstract: Take Back Your Time and various advocacy groups say too many workers feel overstressed by demands on their time and are arguing for more paid time off to allow them to devote more time to their families; Democrats plan to introduce bill guaranteeing seven paid days off each year for when workers or children are ill; Pres Bush and allies favor twice-failed bill offering overtime workers choice between  Middle East enters uncharted political and social territory with mixture of anticipation and dread following opening of Egyptian elections to multiple candidates fall of Lebanese government before street demonstrators and limited vote for municipal councils in Saudi Arabia ; uncensored satellite television spurs protests and pressure from Bush administration emboldens them; American campaign for democracy in Middle East is viewed by many as anti-Arab conspiracy; photo (M)

2005-03-06.title: Torn From Moorings, Villagers From Sri Lanka Grasp for Past
2005-03-06.abstract: Navalady in eastern Sri Lanka, lost at least 620 of about 1,900 inhabitants in tsunami, including wife and four sons of bodybuilder Santosh Chinnathambi Selvam; most of 490 families were related in one way or another; residents waver between vowing never to return and saying they would return if everyone else did; community spirit frayed during refuge at school in Batticaloa, yet according to Hindu custom 30 days after disaster, people returned to Navalady to cook feast for their relatives who had died; photos; map (L)

2005-03-06.title: Sinn Fein Leader Reasserts Rejection of Criminal Activity
2005-03-06.abstract: Sinn Fein leader Gerry Adams uses keynote address at party's annual conference to reiterate party's rejection of criminal activity after months of accusations linking Irish Republican Army and Sinn Fein to bank robberies, money-laundering and murder; promises justice to family of murdered man Robert McCartney although intimidation has stopped all 72 witnesses from giving evidence to police; speech is largely devoted to criticism of Britain, Ireland and Protestant rivals in Northern Ireland (M)

2005-03-07.title: China slaps at U.S. on North Korea
Taiwan Police Say Who Shot President, but Suspect Is Dead
2005-03-07.abstract: Police in Taiwan say they identified man who shot and injured Pres Chen Shui-bian on eve of national elections year ago; say he drowned himself days after shooting, leaving suicide note that blamed Chen for his financial problems but did not take responsibility for shooting; national sympathy for Chen after shooting helped him win re-election; opposition party figures cast doubt on police story and say they will hold demonstration on anniversary of attack (M)

2005-03-07.title: Political fight over retirement system intensifies
U.S. Killing of Italian Officer Stokes Anger Against War
2005-03-07.abstract: Anger is growing in Italy over killing of Italian intelligence officer by American military in Iraq; pressure on Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi to withdraw Italy's 3,000 troops in Iraq is expected to mount; murkiness of details about shooting is also stoking anger; photo (M)

2005-03-07.title: Hostage was warned about GIs, she says

2005-03-08.title: U.S. Opens Inquiry Into Killing of Italian in Iraq
Hans Bethe, Prober of Sunlight and Atomic Energy, Dies at 98
2005-03-08.abstract: Hans A Bethe, who discovered violent reactions behind sunlight, led theoreticians who devised atom bomb and then fought many new weapons of cold war, dies at age 98, last of giants who inaugurated nuclear age; Bethe, who fled Germany in 1930's, pioneered some of most esoteric realms of physics and astrophysics, long advising federal government and in time emerging as science community's liberal conscience; 1938 paper on how sun and similar stars fuse hydrogen into helium, releasing bursts of energy and light, helped establish his reputation as father of nuclear astrophysics and earned him Nobel Prize in 1967; photos; diagram; Bethe was last of greats who led Manhattan Project, outliving not only his conservative counterpoint Edward Teller but also Enrico Fermi and J Robert Oppenheimer (M)

2005-03-08.title: Next Step in Rebuilding Iraq: Bring Power to the People
2005-03-08.abstract: Al Ameen electrical substation in eastern Baghdad is dedicated, reflecting new US and Iraqi focus on individual neighborhoods after nearly two years of concentrating on enormous power plants and high-tension cables; Al Ameen distribution center that will siphon energy from national grid and spread it around districts where aging equipment constantly breaks down (M)

2005-03-08.title: Bush Nominates Weapons Expert as Envoy to U.N.
2005-03-08.abstract: Pres Bush chooses John R Bolton, blunt-spoken conservative known for sharp skepticism of UN, as US ambassador to world body; photo; Sec Condoleezza Rice says he is tough-minded diplomat with track record of effective multilateralism who will speak with strong voice, as did some of well-known predecessors; 56-year-old lawyer has worked in federal government for most of last 25 years, lately as under secretary of state for arms control and international security; choice is lauded by conservatives and criticized by UN diplomats; Vice Pres Cheney reportedly pushed him for post (M)

2005-03-09.title: Violence Continues to Ripple Through Iraq
Israeli Report Condemns Support for Settlement Outposts
2005-03-09.abstract: Long-awaited official report on Israeli government's support for illegal settlements in West Bank describes widespread state complicity, fraud and cynicism, illegal diversion of funds and seizure of private Palestinian land; report by former chief prosecutor Talia Sasson was commissioned under US pressure and delivered to Prime Min Ariel Sharon, whose government is accused of 'blatant violations of law' in helping settlers set up illegal outposts (M)

2005-03-09.title: World Briefing | Asia: Steps To A Tsunami Warning System
2005-03-09.abstract: United Nations Intergovernmental Oceanographic Commission reports progress in development of interenational tsunami warning system; basic alert system is expected to be operating in Indian Ocean by fall 2005 and final system in place by end of 2006 (S)

2005-03-09.title: Day After Report, Author Says Israeli Democracy Is at Risk
Hostage rescue had U.S. permission, says Berlusconi

2005-03-10.title: Rebel's Death Stirs Debate on Strategy for Chechnya
2005-03-10.abstract: Politicians loyal to Kremlin hail killing of Chechen rebel leader Aslan Maskhadov as demoralizing loss to separatists; human rights organizations see loss of both willing negotiator and moderating influence over separatists, many of whom have turned to terrorism in recent years; warn greater violence may be in store; separatists set three-day mourning period and jihad to avenge his death (M)

2005-03-10.title: Showdown in Britain over antiterror laws
IOC inspectors dodge strikes as they tour Paris
Letter from Japan: A lasting anger at Japan
U.S. sends formal complaint to BA

2005-03-11.title: Vietnamese lose in court on defoliant
Even as it pulls back, Syria tightens grip
World Briefing | Americas: Bolivia: Thousands Cheer President
2005-03-11.abstract: Bolivian Pres Carlos Mesa, who has faced large protests against his policies, celebrates in La Paz with thousands of supporters; calls for dialogue with opposition to resolve conflict (S)

2005-03-11.title: Islamic Court in Pakistan Reinstates Convictions in Rape Case
Spain Pays Solemn Tribute to Victims of Train Blasts

2005-03-12.title: World Briefing | Africa: Congo: U.N. In New Operation Against Militiamen
2005-03-12.abstract: UN soldiers in eastern Congo conduct major operation to find and disarm tribal fighters who have been terrorizing local population, but largely come up empty (S)

2005-03-12.title: Europe and U.S. Agree on Carrot-and-Stick Approach to Iran
2005-03-12.abstract: Europe's leading nations, yielding to US demands for tougher stance on Iran, warn that any failure by Iranian government to give up its suspected nuclear arms program would leave them 'no choice' but to seek punishments at UN Security Council; Sec of State Condoleezza Rice confirms that US has offered limited economic incentives to Iran if it cooperated on nuclear matters (M)

2005-03-12.title: World Briefing | Americas: Venezuela: Court Reverses 2002 Coup Ruling
2005-03-12.abstract: Venezuela Atty Gen Isaias Rodriguez promises new prosecutions against government opponents believed to have supported brief overthrow of Pres Hugo Chavez in 2002; Supreme Court reverses ruling that found that four military officers accused of leading coup could not be put on trial (S)

2005-03-12.title: Musharraf gesture stirs hopes for peace

2005-03-13.title: Pope to be discharged from hospital
Names of the Dead
2005-03-13.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of one American sailor in Iraq, bringing total to 1,510 American servicemen dead

2005-03-13.title: American Doctors Helped Identify Ukraine Leader's Poisoning
2005-03-13.abstract: Team of American doctors from University of Virginia flew secretly to Vienna in mid-December to help care for poisoned Ukrainian presidential candidate Viktor A Yushchenko at invitation of Austrian physicians supervising his treatment; European laboratories identified agent as highly toxic form of dioxin; American involvement was kept secret in part at request of Yushchenko, whose opponents criticized him for being too pro-American (M)

2005-03-13.title: Looting at Weapons Plants Was Systematic, Iraqi Says
2005-03-13.abstract: Iraqi Deputy Minister of Industry Sami al-Araji says after fall of Baghdad looters systematically dismantled and removed tons of machinery from most important weapons installations, including some with high-precision equipment capable of making parts for nuclear arms; facilities were left largely unguarded by allied forces; peak of organized looting was from mid-April to mid-May of 2003 as teams with flatbed trucks and heavy equipment moved systematically from site to site; United Nations has been hunting for equipment unsuccessfully across Middle East; photos; map (L)

2005-03-13.title: A Cricket Match Bridges a Longtime Gap in Punjab
2005-03-13.abstract: As India and Pakistan face off in Mohali, India, for first test match of six-week cricket series, Indians and Pakistanis greet each other with mixture of emotions; Pakistani Pres Pervez Musharraf is expected to attend a match, probably in Indian city of Kochi, adding explicitly political flavor to game; last year's match took Indian team to Pakistan and prompted effusion of hospitality; this year's match occurs amid peace talks that have led to cooperation between two countries on Kashmir issue; photos; map (M)

2005-03-14.title: Lebanon Needs to Act First For Syria to Exit, Envoy Says
2005-03-14.abstract: Terje Roed-Larson, special United Nations envoy on Lebanon, cautions that commitment by Syrian Pres Bashar al-Assad to withdraw all troops from Lebanon in next few months is contingent on formation of new government by Lebanon's leaders, task that is likely to be extremely difficult because of turbulent politics; Syria and Lebanon are to meet on April 7 to set date for withdrawal; photos (M)

2005-03-14.title: Attacks kill 3 in Iraq over weekend
Chain Saw Thins Flocks of Migrants on Gold Wings
2005-03-14.abstract: Colony of monarch butterflies that arrived in Contepec, Mexico, in November was tiny and retreated up mountains as far as possible from lower slopes where loggers have thinned or destroyed forest butterflies depend on; number arriving this winter was smallest since Mexico and World Wildlife Fund began keeping records in 1970's, down three-quarters from winter before; biologists and nature lovers blame logging in Mexico and herbicides in US for diminished numbers; photos; map (M)

2005-03-14.title: Pope Returns Home After 18 Days in Hospital
2005-03-14.abstract: Pope John Paul II leaves hospital and returns to Vatican, making sign of cross from car to hundreds of well-wishers lining hospital complex; photo (M)

2005-03-14.title: Cries of Fraud Give Election in Kyrgyzstan Aura of Ukraine
2005-03-14.abstract: Kyrgyzstan holds most fractious elections in its post-Soviet history, marked by protests and accusations of fraud; reports of vote buying and efforts to intimidate press in first round of elections for Parliament resulted in public anger; critics of Pres Askar Akayev hoped that his handpicked successor might be defeated in presidential election coming in fall; Kumanbek Bakiyev is considered most likely to defeat Akayev or designated successor; map (M)

2005-03-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: 4 Sentenced In Milan Airport Collision
2005-03-15.abstract: Four employees of ENAV, air traffic control company for 39 Italian airports, are sentenced to up to four years in prison for manslaughter stemming from 2001 Linate airport collision that killed 118 people (S)

2005-03-15.title: North Korea impasse to be focus of Rice trip
Halliburton Overcharged$108 Million, Report Says
2005-03-15.abstract: Pentagon auditors find that excess billing for postwar fuel imports to Iraq by Halliburton Co totaled more than $108 million; report was completed last fall, but has never been officially released to public or to Congress; Rep Henry A Waxman wants stronger effort to reclaim money from Halliburton; photo (M)

2005-03-15.title: Hong Kong successor to serve just 2 years, Tsang says
How Electronics Are Penetrating North Korea's Isolation
2005-03-15.abstract: Consumer electronics introduce new ways of thinking into North Korea, perhaps corroding steely controls on ideology and information that have kept Kim family in power for almost 60 years; defectors from North Korea say that construction of cellular relay stations last fall along Chinese side of border allows some North Koreans in border towns to use prepaid Chinese cellphones to call relatives and reporters in South Korea; after DVD players swept northern China two years ago, entrepreneurs collected castoff videocassette recorders and peddled them to North Korea; tapes of South Korean soap operas are popular; photo (M)

2005-03-16.title: Bush confidante to work on improving U.S. image
School ban on scarves wins praise
New Museum, Putting a Human Face on the Holocaust, Opens in Israel
2005-03-16.abstract: More than 40 heads of state and cabinet ministers, many from Europe, gather in Jerusalem for opening of new Holocaust museum at Yad Vashem; exhibits try to put human face on unimaginable horror, telling story of six million Jewish dead, names of half still unknown, through personal diaries, images and testimonies; photo; Prime Min Ariel Sharon, UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan and others speak; Mayor Michael Bloomberg leads US delegation (M)

2005-03-16.title: Handover of West Bank town of Jericho begins
Iraqis Say Italians Aren't Cooperating in Kidnapping Investigation
2005-03-16.abstract: Iraqi investigators trying to find kidnappers of Italian journalist Giuliana Sgrena are stymied by lack of cooperation from Italian intelligence services that won her release by negotiating with abductors; police official Jabbar Anwar warns kidnappings by criminal ring will continue, especially if ransom is paid, as reported in Italian media; suspicious circumstances surrounding Sgrena seizure recounted; photo (M)

2005-03-17.title: World Briefing | Europe: Europeans Halt Croatia Entry Talks
2005-03-17.abstract: European Union postpones membership talks with Croatia; holds that Croatia is being uncooperative with United Nations war crimes tribunal since it has not captured and turned over Ante Gotovina, former Croatian general indicted by tribunal (S)

2005-03-17.title: U.S. Envoy Leaves Iraq, Conceding 'Job Is Far From Done'
Palestinians Gain Security Control of Jericho
2005-03-17.abstract: Israeli troops transfer security control to Palestinians in Jericho, West Bank town that has been nearly free of violence in recent years; move is largely symbolic but points to prospect of Israeli pullback from four other West Bank towns that Israel agreed in principle to do after truce with Palestinians was announced five weeks ago; photo; map (M)

2005-03-17.title: Manila fears attack after prison toll

2005-03-18.title: World Briefing | Africa: Somalia: Lawmakers Brawl Over Peacekeepers
2005-03-18.abstract: Meeting in Nairobi, Kenya, of members of Somalia's exiled Parliament ends up in brawl over makeup of peacekeeping force; one member of Parliament is seriously injured (S)

2005-03-18.title: Indonesia to proceed with pollution case against U.S. company
A Haircut in Iraq Can Be the Death of the Barber
2005-03-18.abstract: Barbers in Iraq live in fear of militant Islamists who are killing those who shave men's beards or do Western-style haircuts; as many as 12 barbers have been killed; many other barbers have received threats; killings and threats are not first efforts in Iraq to enforce Taliban-style religious rule; in Falluja, armed mujahedeen ran Islamic police state for months before American invasion, and in southern Iraq, Shiite militants attacked liquor stores and sometimes killed those who ignored their threats; killings and threats in Baghdad are concentrated in Doura, working-class neighborhood that has seen so much violence that police say there is little they can do to protect barbers; photos (M)

2005-03-18.title: Ex-Halliburton Man Charged With Defrauding U.S. of $3.5 Million
2005-03-18.abstract: Former employee of Halliburton subsidiary Kellogg Brown & Root, Jeff Alex Mazon, is indicted along with Kuwaiti businessman, charged with defrauding United States of more than $3.5 million in subcontract to supply fuel tankers for military operations in Iraq; Mazon allegedly received bribe from Kuwaiti, Ali Hajazi, for helping his firm reap $5.5 million from contract that should have cost $2 million; Mazon has been arrested while Hijazi has not been apprehended; Halliburton claims it discovered evidence of crime and notified Justice Dept (M)

2005-03-19.title: In Life on the Mekong, China's Dams Dominate
2005-03-19.abstract: Last of three articles in series China's Reach notes that China's appetite for hydroelectric power and its thrust into Southeast Asia in search of trade are changing the character of Mekong River, not only in China, but also for five nations and 60 million rural people downstream; China has built two dams, and plans to build six more along upper Mekong; conservationists say water levels and temperatures have fluctuated widely, threatening river environment and disrupting livelihoods of fishermen and others who depend on $2 billion annual catch of migratory fish; changes to river that China has already made are quickly causing way of life to recede in Myanmar, Thailand, Laos, Cambodia and Vietnam; map; photos (M)

2005-03-19.title: Seeking the roots of ill health
Mercy killing of infants: A doctor explains
World Briefing | Europe: Iceland: Citizenship Close For Bobby Fischer
2005-03-19.abstract: Special parliamentary committee recommends that Bobby Fischer, US chess grandmaster, be given Icelandic citizenship; Fischer has been in custody in Japan fighting deportation to US, where he is wanted for violating economic sanctions against former Yugoslavia by traveling there to play chess in 1992 (S)

2005-03-19.title: India scoop: For viewers or voyeurs?

2005-03-20.title: U.S. Avoids Role of Mediator as Iraqis Remain Deadlocked
2005-03-20.abstract: Senior Bush administration officials, having concluded that any American effort to mediate would likely backfire, avoid direct intervention to break deadlock among Sunni, Shiite and Kurdish factions who are still trying to form government six weeks after national elections; United States resists call from Kurds, Shiites and some Arab neighbors of Iraq to play facilitating role; Kurdish dispute with Shiites tends to overshadow parallel problem of bringing Sunnis into government (M)

2005-03-20.title: Putin Makes Good-Will Visit to Ukraine President
Humphrey Spender, Lens to British Poor, Dies at 94
2005-03-20.abstract: Humphrey Spender, prominent English artist known for his photographs documenting lives of ordinary Britons during Depression, dies at age 94; photo (M)

2005-03-20.title: Pope Misses Palm Sunday Services for First Time

2005-03-21.title: Wolfowitz Choice May Lead to a New Nomination Process
After 5 years of softer rule, Syria's leader moves to consolidate power
U.S. warns Iraq on new government
In Beijing, Rice keeps focus on N.Korea
Iraqi policehit by wave of attacks

2005-03-22.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: 3,000 Seek Refuge After Quake
2005-03-22.abstract: One person is killed and 735 others injured after southern Japan earthquake; 3,000 people take refuge in local gymnasiums; Genkaijima is hardest hit with most homes being damaged or destroyed (S)

2005-03-22.title: Iceland Granting Citizenship to Bobby Fischer, Held in Japan
2005-03-22.abstract: Iceland's Parliament votes to grant citizenship to American chess star Bobby Fischer; supporters say this will lay groundwork for his release from Japanese prison where he has been held since last summer, fighting deportation to United States; he has been in legal limbo since 1992 when, US says, he violated sanctions against Yugoslavia by accepting $3.3 million fee to play exhibition match there; in Iceland, where he defeated Russian world champion Boris Spassky in 1972 match in Reykjavik, he is seen as national hero (M)

2005-03-22.title: U.S. Catholic bishops taking on death penalty
High court won't rule in terror suspect case
Qaeda suspects admit plans to attack Western targets

2005-03-23.title: World Briefing | Africa: Nigeria: High-Level Bribery Scandal
2005-03-23.abstract: Nigerian Pres Olusegun Obasanjo fires Education Minister Fabian Osuji and accuses Senate Pres Adolphus Wabara of taking bribe; holds that fighting government corruption is government's top priority (S)

2005-03-23.title: Mall bomb brings Lebanon closer to chaos
Nuclear Power Is Good: U.S. and Iran Have No Argument There
2005-03-23.abstract: Amb Constance Morella, speaking for Energy Sec Samuel Bodman, affirms United States's strong support for nuclear energy, speech, Paris, to conference on nuclear energy; cites Pres Bush's call to resume building nuclear plants in US; warns civilian nuclear power must not be diverted to arms programs; Iranian nuclear official Mohammad Saeidi also strongly backs nuclear energy; reaffirms that Iran will develop full-scale nuclear program and not abandon its right to enrich uranium, never mentioning its temporary freeze on enrichment efforts and talks with France, Britain and Germany on permanent freeze (M)

2005-03-23.title: Egypt charges opposition chief with forgery
World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Singer's Ex-Manager Guilty Of Rape
2005-03-23.abstract: Chihuahua, Mexico, judge finds Sergio Andrade, former manager and husband of singer Gloria Trevi, guilty of rape, kidnapping and corruption of minor girl and sentences him to more than seven years in prison; Andrade has already served five years since his arrest in Brazil along with Trevi; all charges were dropped against Trevi last year (S)

2005-03-24.title: Swedes are soft on terrorism, Russians say
Virus New to Angola Kills 95; Travelers Told to Avoid North
2005-03-24.abstract: Health official urge travelers to avoid Angola's Uige Province after World Health Organization identifies Marburg virus, close relative of Ebola, as source of epidemic that has killed at least 95 Angolans since Oct; virus, previously identified in Uganda, Kenya and Congo, is new to Angola; United Nations officials suspend repatriation program under which tens of thousands of refugees from Angola's 21-year civil war have returned from Congo through Uige as evidence of disease spreads; Angola has begun public awareness drive stressing need for cleanliness (M)

2005-03-24.title: Best-selling author's life in the fishbowl
Conference of Arab Leaders Yields Little of Significance
2005-03-24.abstract: Arab leaders end poorly attended two-day Arab League summit conference, Algiers, with little to show for it; critics complain that resolutions passed are of comparatively little significance; leaders give little mention to Arab political reform and divisions, even though visitors like Spain's Prime Min Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero, French Min Michel Barnier and United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan prod them to discuss political reforms; use conference as forum for personal squabbles (M)

2005-03-24.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Police Break Up Fake Euro Ring
2005-03-24.abstract: Spanish police officials announce arrest of 27 people in connection with largest counterfeiting effort to be discovered in Spain since 2002 introduction of Euro; seized 2.5 million counterfeit euros during arrests (S)

2005-03-25.title: North American nations unite to compete with Asia on trade
10,000 Peacekeepers to Be Sent to Sudan, U.N. Council Decides
2005-03-25.abstract: Security Council unanimously passes resolution introduced by United States that would establish 10,000-member peacekeeping force for Sudan to reinforce peace agreement in south of country and to lend assistance in conflicted Darfur region in west; passage occurs after France postpones consideration of resolution that would refer war crime cases from Sudan to International Criminal Court, move seen as challenge to US and likely to provoke American veto; council members disagree over placing sanctions on Sudan, and on which court should try perpetrators of atrocities in light of American objections to international court (M)

2005-03-25.title: Report Calls for Punishing Peacekeepers in Sex Abuse
2005-03-25.abstract: Report on sexual abuse by United Nations peacekeepers in Congo recommends that offending soldiers and their commanders be punished by their home countries, that payments made to them be recovered and put into fund for victims and that UN make compliance with these measures condition for talking part in its missions; Secretary General Kofi Annan commissioned report after evidence emerged that blue helmeted peacekeepers and civilian staff members had sex with women and girls in Congo in exchange for food and money. and in some instances raped them; report also recommends that assistance be provided for 'peacekeeper babies' left behind when troops rotate back to their own countries; report was prepared by Prince Zeid Raad al Hussein, Jordan's ambassador to UN who is former military officer and civilian peacekeeper (M)

2005-03-25.title: U.N. Cites Syria as Factor in Lebanese Assassination
2005-03-25.abstract: Toughly worded United Nations report into assassination of former Lebanese prime minister Rafik Hariri concludes that heavy-handed Syrian interference in Lebanese affairs created polarizing tensions that led to his death and that deeply flawed local investigation obstructed efforts to find his killers; report, by Patrick Fitzgerald, deputy police commissioner of Ireland, calls for investigation by independent commission as only way of uncovering truth behind Harari's killing; Secretary General Kofi Annan endorses call for international investigation; Fitzgerald's report notes that Lebanese security services ignored threats on Hariri's life, and that his security detail was cut to 8 people from 40 after he left office; photo (M)

2005-03-26.title: Two U.S. Soldiers, Marine Killed in Iraq
Parents press appeal as denials continue
Vital Signs of a Ruined Falluja Grow Stronger
2005-03-26.abstract: Some signs of life are returning to Falluja four months after US bombs and guns pounded much of Iraqi city into ruins; about third of city's 250,000 residents have trickled back since early Jan; safety has come at high price, and high security has stunted renascent economy and reconstruction effort; map; photos (M)

2005-03-26.title: INDIA IS CRITICAL
2005-03-26.abstract: US will sell F-16 jet fighters to Pakistan in deal that State Dept says will improve regional security; size of arms sale has not been decided, but Pakistan is seeking about two dozen planes; Pres Bush telephones Prime Min Manmohan Singh of India to inform him of decision; clears way for India to discuss its own purchase of combat aircraft; Singh says arms sale will undermine regional security (M)

2005-03-26.title: U.S. agrees to sell F-16 jet fighters to Pakistan

2005-03-27.title: Pope Makes Appearance but Is Unable to Speak
Bomb in Christian Area of Beirut Wounds 5
2005-03-27.abstract: Car bomb explodes in predominantly Christian Sid al Bushriya neighborhood in eastern Beirut on eve of Easter, wounding at least five people and destroying two factories in third attack on a Christian neighborhood in week; Lebanon's Christians are overwhelmingly opposed to Syria's military occupation, and many Lebanese drawn to site of bombing say they suspect Syrian involvement; photo (M)

2005-03-27.title: Lord Callaghan, British Prime Minister for 3 Tumultuous Years, Is Dead at 92
2005-03-27.abstract: James Callaghan, elder statesman of British Labor Party whose three years as prime minister were marked by infamous 'Winter of Discontent' and tumultuous battles with labor unions, dies at age 92; photo (M)

2005-03-27.title: Cause of Mystery Ills Splits Indonesian Fishing Village
2005-03-27.abstract: People of Buyat Bay region on island of Sulawesi in Indonesia are divided on cause of mysterious lumps, skin rashes, dizziness and other ailments afflicting them; some blame pollution from nearby goldmine operated by Denver-based Newmont Mining Corp and demand government relocate them; government recently announced $117 million suit against Newmont, and six Newmont executives, including two Americans, face criminal charges; environmental groups and Newmont mobilize opposing camps i n Buyat Bay, deepening animosity; photos, map (L)

2005-03-27.title: U.S. Soldiers Discover Tunnel at Detention Camp
2005-03-27.abstract: American soldiers guarding sprawling southern detention center of Camp Bucca in Iraq discover largest tunnel so far found at a detention center, 12 to 16 feet underground and running 600 feet to exterior of camp; tunnel was apparently discovered before any detainees had chance to use it; it is unclear how many of camp's 6,049 detainees worked on tunnel; detainees rioted on January 31; guards began searching for escape passages after smaller tunnel was found last week; pho to (M)

2005-03-28.title: White House Letter: New Bush? Aides say he's just feeling good
Iraqis fire on protesters, killing one
Japan tells Chirac 'no' on embargo's end
UN leader faces criticism in independent report
Israel Says Palestinians Abet Smuggling of Antiaircraft Missiles

2005-03-29.title: Kyrgyz in Political Compromise
2005-03-29.abstract: Politicians and lawmakers in Kyrguzstan resolve brewing parliamentary crisis when interim prime minister Kurmanbek Bakiyev says he would recognize new legislators who won seats in the flawed elections that led to widespread protests and drove Pres Askar A Akayev from power; some officials say they are neogating with Akayev to formally resign, to allow for smoother transfer of power; Akayev sends statement to official news agency demanding end to what he calls persecution of his friends and relatives and warning that foreign investment would dry up as result of 'anarchy' in country; says nothing of resigning (M)

2005-03-29.title: Iraq rebels kill 8; assembly prepares to choose speaker
Talks on Baghdad Power-Sharing Drag On
2005-03-29.abstract: Iraq's leading political parties negotiate furiously, but apparently fruitlessly, on day before scheduled meeting of national assembly; wave of violence sweeps area south of Baghdad known as Triangle of Death for regularity with which Iraqi civilians and security personnel are gunned down; at least 9 people are killed; attacks are expected to increase as Shiite pilgrims head through area to Karbala for religious festival; Pres Traian Basescu of Romania says three Romanian journalists were abducted; photo (M)

2005-03-30.title: Wolfowitz Seems to Win European Support for World Bank Nomination
Canadian sues U.S. over trip to Syria
World Briefing | Africa: Uganda: Protest Over Polygamy Bill
2005-03-30.abstract: Polygamy bill before Ugandan Parliament, which would require men to get permission of their first wife before taking another, is met with protest by Muslims, who say polygamy is religious not legislative matter (S)

2005-03-31.title: Rogue Fans Disrupt Iran-North Korea Soccer Match
2005-03-31.abstract: Angry North Korean soccer fans attending second half of World Cup qualifying match in stadium in Pyongyang pelt visiting players from Iran and referees with bottles and cans, requiring intervention of police and providing seldom seen evidence of spontaneous violence in tightly controlled state; incident begins as North Korean team trails Iran, and lasts for hour after match ends with North Korea's loss, 2-0; photos (M)

2005-03-31.title: In Indonesian Quake's Aftermath, Inconsolable Grief
2005-03-31.abstract: In aftermath of latest earthquake in Indonesian island Nias, people are still lying under piles of rubble as their neighbors, with almost no heavy equipment, tear with their hands at blocks of concrete and piles of brick; so far just few hundreds bodies have been counted, but officials agree that death toll on Nias and islands closest to it could reach 1,000; because of its remoteness, little food, shelter or assistance have yet arrived on Nias; aid workers say so much material was stockpiled on mainland after tsunami in Dec that aid will be plentiful once flow gets under way; some residents recall horror of quake; photo; map (M)

2005-03-31.title: Study Faults U.S. Response to Outlawed Arms
2005-03-31.abstract: Presidential commission on United States intelligence concludes that government has failed to respond to dire threat posed by unconventional weapons with great enough urgency and national purpose; report gives grim account of spy agencies' capabilities, despite steady increase in intelligence budget since 9/11 attacks; this is latest of several scathing assessments of intelligence failures; succession of reports designed to fix blame for botched intelligence on 9/11 attacks and Iraqi weapons has generated some wariness and cynicism at agencies (M)

2005-03-31.title: Taiwan Opposition Welcomed in China
2005-03-31.abstract: China's government welcomes high-ranking delegation from Taiwan's opposition Nationalist Party to Beijing, even as top Taiwanese official in charge of mainland relations denounces visit, accusing opposition of helping Chinese Communist Party at expense of Taiwan (S)
